subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – For Anne Gregory
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given lines.

passages.paragraph: In the poem 'For Anne Gregory', a young man speaks about Anne Gregory’s beauty. He says that her hair is bright yellow and very attractive. Because of this beauty, young men admire her. He believes that people are attracted to her mainly because of her appearance rather than her true personality.
questions: Who speaks about Anne Gregory’s beauty in the poem?
What colour is Anne Gregory’s hair?
Why do young men admire Anne Gregory?
What does the young man believe about people's attraction?
What idea about beauty is suggested in this passage?

passages.paragraph: The young man also describes Anne Gregory’s hair as 'honey-coloured ramparts'. This phrase creates a strong image of golden hair surrounding her face. According to him, her beauty is so striking that it becomes difficult for anyone to see beyond her outward appearance.
questions: What phrase is used to describe Anne Gregory’s hair?
What does 'honey-coloured ramparts' refer to?
What image does this phrase create?
Why does the young man think people cannot see beyond her beauty?
Which feature of Anne Gregory attracts attention?

passages.paragraph: Anne Gregory listens to the young man and responds thoughtfully. She says that if her hair is the reason people admire her, she could easily change it. She suggests that she can dye her hair brown, black, or carrot. By doing so, she hopes that someone might love her for her true self rather than for her appearance.
questions: How does Anne Gregory respond to the young man’s claim?
What solution does she suggest?
Which hair colours does she mention?
Why does she want to change her hair colour?
What does Anne Gregory wish people would value?

passages.paragraph: Anne Gregory’s reply shows her desire to be loved for her inner personality. She believes that true love should recognise a person’s character rather than physical beauty. Her argument challenges the common belief that beauty determines attraction.
questions: What does Anne Gregory want people to recognise?
What does she believe true love should value?
Which common belief does she challenge?
What idea about personality is presented in this passage?
How does this passage question superficial attraction?

passages.paragraph: In the final part of the poem, an old religious man is mentioned. He declares that only God can love someone for themselves alone. According to him, human beings are often influenced by outward beauty. This idea highlights the difference between human love and divine love.
questions: Who is mentioned in the final part of the poem?
What does the religious man declare?
According to him, who can love someone for themselves alone?
What influence affects human love?
What contrast between two types of love is shown here?
